Chemistry Lesson
Topic: Acid-Base Reactions
Class: SSS1

The teacher explains what acids and bases are. Students should know that acids have pH below 7 and bases have pH above 7.

David helps distribute the pH indicator strips to the class. Sarah records results. Robert mixes solutions carefully.

The class identifies: lemon juice is acidic, soap is basic, water is neutral.

Students list common household acids and bases.

Home work: Define neutralisation and identify three examples from everyday life.
reference: NERDC SSS1 Chemistry lesson on acid-base reactions where students design and conduct experiments, construct pH scales, and create a neutralisation reference chart.
subject: chemistry
notes: Three failures: (1) CREATE expected but uses REMEMBER verbs (explain, know, identify, list, define) — no design/create/construct/develop verbs. (2) No Objective/Activity/Assessment labels — format_compliance fails. (3) Western names throughout (David, Sarah, Robert) — cultural_flag_check fails.
